findings.summary: Diagnostics history card is fixture/deferred and explicitly requires a bounded redacted JSON command surface before live history is admitted.
UI switches fixture/live display state and renders sanitized artifact metadata; it does not read bundle contents or raw files.
Live server uses strict JSON command packets, records forbidden-field metadata, and normalizes diagnostics export bundle references.
Negative tests forbid browser storage, file-reading, browser file picker, and related unsafe UI behavior.

risks.summary: Richer live diagnostics history remains not admitted until a new bounded packet command surface exists.
